name: Folium (Interactive Maps with Leaflet.js)

auth.notes: Local library; tile provider API keys optional (MapBox, Google)

performance.notes: Browser-rendered via Leaflet.js; struggles above 10k points without clustering

agent_readiness.notes: Python-to-Leaflet.js interactive map generator. import folium. m = folium.Map(location=[51.5, -0.1], zoom_start=12). folium.Marker([51.5, -0.1], popup='London', icon=folium.Icon(color='red')).add_to(m). folium.CircleMarker().add_to(m). GeoJson overlay: folium.GeoJson(gdf).add_to(m). Choropleth: folium.Choropleth(geo_data=..., data=df, columns=['id', 'value']). m.save('map.html') — standalone HTML. Plugins: HeatMap, MarkerCluster, TimestampedGeoJson. Jupyter display: m (returns map inline). Multiple tile providers.

gotchas: Performance degrades above 10k markers — use MarkerCluster or pydeck
Generated HTML requires internet connection for tile loading
best_when: Interactive choropleth maps for data stories
Sharing interactive maps as standalone HTML files
avoid_when: Large point datasets (>10k) — use pydeck with WebGL
Production web maps requiring performance (use Leaflet.js directly)
summary: Folium generates interactive Leaflet.js maps from Python. m = folium.Map(location=[lat, lon], zoom_start=12). folium.Marker(), CircleMarker(), GeoJson(), Choropleth(). Plugins: HeatMap, MarkerCluster, TimestampedGeoJson. m.save('map.html') for standalone HTML or m display in Jupyter. Integrates with GeoPandas DataFrames. Best for choropleth maps and moderate-density point maps. For large datasets (>10k points) use pydeck with WebGL rendering.
